title: eriogonum trichopes subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum trichopes subsp. typicum, also known as the Nevada buckwheat or Nevada buckwheat sagebrush, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of this plant are believed to provide relief from wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, as well as exhibit antioxidant and antiviral properties. Eriogonum trichopes subsp. typicum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically in the form of salves and ointments.
